# Test fixtures — provenance

Read this before you trust a fixture. Two of these are real captures; the rest are not. Mistaking one for the other is how a renderer gets built against invented data.

## Real-captured (ground truth)

| File | Source | Shape |
|---|---|---|
| `manifest-weseeyou.json` | `agent-access.json` at the root of the WeSeeYouAtTheMovies app, generated by vibe-access v0.1.0 (`scan` → `map` → `verify`, runId `c9643359`) | 85 affordances, `firebase-functions` adapter, HTTP. 84 of 85 descriptions are the `Act:/Read:` template; 1 (`agent-seed`) is authored. `input`/`output` are `null` on all 85. Verify details: 62 `auth-gate-held`, 14 unstamped, 7 `unexpected 500`, 1 `unexpected 404`, 1 `auth-gate-open`. |
| `manifest-rororo.json` | `agent-access.json` at the root of the ROROROblox app, generated by vibe-access v0.1.0 (runId `b9972f14`) | 17 affordances, `agnostic:dotnet-wpf-desktop` adapter. gRPC over a Windows named pipe wearing a `transport.type: "http"` label — the honest-transport case §2.1 exists for. Every affordance carries an authored `overrides.description`. |

**Scrub applied:** both files were walked field-by-field for secrets, bearer tokens, JWTs, API keys, email addresses, and absolute local paths. **Zero hits** — the manifests carry none of that. They are therefore committed byte-faithful apart from JSON re-serialization (2-space indent, trailing newline). `sourceRef` values are repo-relative and stay repo-relative (Windows backslashes preserved as captured). `baseUrls` are `http://localhost:5000` and `npipe://./pipe/rororo-plugin-host` — local, no hosts leaked.

The point of keeping them real: the 84/85 template ratio, the 12-of-17 capability mining, and the verify class decomposition (`3 ran, 12 gate-held, 2 handle-gate-held`) are assertions against *observed* data. Synthesize the data and the assertions test the synthesizer.

## Faithfully reconstructed (NOT a live capture)

| File | What it is |
|---|---|
| `mcp-tools-list.json` | An MCP `tools/list` result modeled on the **real 626Labs MCP server's tool surface** (tool names, discriminated `action` enums, and semantics are the server's), but the payload was **hand-written to the MCP spec's result shape, not captured off the wire**. Ten tools. Deliberately includes the two ragged real-world cases the renderer must survive: `verify_gold_standard` (thin one-line description, **no `inputSchema` at all**) and `get_mcp_instructions` (**no description**, `inputSchema` with empty `properties`). Do not cite counts from this file as facts about the 626Labs server. |
| `reference-626-manifest.json` | Pre-existing synthetic manifest from v0.1.0. Invented, not captured. |

If someone later captures a true `tools/list` frame off the 626Labs connector, replace `mcp-tools-list.json` with it and move its row into the table above.
